description: Evaluates SkyPilot bootstrap behavior and high-value operational failure modes for agents

evals.name: happy-path-server-connected
evals.prompt: I want to launch an 8xH100 cluster for training a LLaMA model. My SkyPilot is already set up.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api info -o json`, sees a reachable server with version/status/user data, skips credential/bootstrap work, then proceeds to help write or launch the H100 task.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api info -o json` as the first SkyPilot command
Agent does NOT run `sky check` when the API server is already connected and no credential diagnosis is needed
Agent does NOT run `sky api start` or `sky api login` when the server is already connected
Agent proceeds to the user's actual launch/YAML task without unnecessary setup detours

evals.name: server-not-running-local-setup
evals.prompt: I just installed SkyPilot and want to try launching a GPU instance on AWS. I don't have a remote API server.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api info -o json`, detects no connected API server, starts a local server with `sky api start`, rechecks with `sky api info -o json`, then runs `sky check -o json` because this is a fresh AWS setup.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api info -o json` as the first SkyPilot command
Agent runs `sky api start` because the user explicitly said they do not have a remote API server
Agent re-runs `sky api info -o json` after starting the local server
Agent runs `sky check -o json` for the fresh AWS credential setup

evals.name: server-not-running-remote-connect
evals.prompt: I need to connect to our team's SkyPilot API server at https://skypilot.internal.company.com and launch a fine-tuning job.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `sky api info -o json`, detects no reachable connected server, uses the provided endpoint with `sky api login -e https://skypilot.internal.company.com`, rechecks with `sky api info -o json`, and proceeds to the fine-tuning task.

assertions.text: Agent runs `sky api info -o json` as the first SkyPilot command
Agent uses `sky api login -e https://skypilot.internal.company.com` because the user provided the endpoint
Agent does NOT ask the user for the server URL again
Agent re-runs `sky api info -o json` after login

evals.name: cluster-job-status-101
evals.prompt: My cluster job might have failed. `sky logs train 7 --status` returned exit code 101 and printed RUNNING.
evals.expected_output: Agent interprets 101 as not finished, not failure. It checks scheduler state with `sky queue train -o json --skip-finished` and suggests recent logs or SSH/GPU/process probes if progress is unclear.

assertions.text: Agent states that exit code 101 means the job is not finished
Agent does NOT treat exit code 101 as a failed job
Agent uses or recommends `sky queue train -o json --skip-finished` to inspect current scheduler state
Agent uses targeted logs or SSH process/GPU checks before declaring the job hung

evals.name: ssh-secret-environment
evals.prompt: I SSHed into my cluster and `echo $WANDB_API_KEY` is empty, but I launched with `--secret WANDB_API_KEY`. Can you debug by running the script manually over SSH?
evals.expected_output: Agent explains that SkyPilot secrets are injected into the task setup/run environment, not arbitrary SSH shells. It avoids exposing the secret and reruns through `sky exec --secret WANDB_API_KEY` or asks for/sources a deliberate remote env file.

assertions.text: Agent says interactive SSH shells do not automatically inherit SkyPilot task setup/run secrets
Agent does NOT ask the user to print or paste the secret value
Agent uses or recommends `sky exec ... --secret WANDB_API_KEY` for commands requiring the secret

evals.name: stale-remote-workdir
evals.prompt: I deleted an old config locally and reran `sky exec`, but the job still seems to use it on the cluster.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes that local-path workdir rsync is additive, inspects `~/sky_workdir`, uses `find` instead of assuming paths, and recommends cleaning the specific stale files or wiping `~/sky_workdir` before rerun when safe.

assertions.text: Agent states that SkyPilot local-path workdir sync can leave deleted local files on the remote
Agent checks or recommends checking `~/sky_workdir` on the cluster
Agent uses or recommends `find ~/sky_workdir` to locate the stale config
Agent does NOT assume the local filesystem state exactly matches the remote filesystem state

evals.name: progress-bar-log-parsing
evals.prompt: The training log has tqdm progress bars and grep/tail output looks contradictory. How should I inspect it?
evals.expected_output: Agent normalizes carriage returns with `tr "\r" "\n"`, uses targeted grep patterns, and warns that progress bars may reset per phase or epoch.

assertions.text: Agent uses or recommends `tr "\r" "\n"` before grepping progress-bar logs
Agent uses targeted grep patterns instead of streaming full logs
Agent warns that progress bars can reset per phase, epoch, shard, or domain

evals.name: silent-long-running-job
evals.prompt: My SkyPilot job has not printed logs for 20 minutes. Should I cancel it?
evals.expected_output: Agent does not cancel immediately. It checks job status, recent targeted logs, `ps`, `nvidia-smi`, and optionally `nvidia-smi pmon -c 1` before deciding whether the job is hung.

assertions.text: Agent does NOT recommend immediate cancellation based only on stale logs
Agent checks or recommends `sky logs CLUSTER JOB_ID --status` and scheduler state
Agent checks or recommends `ps` and `nvidia-smi` over SSH
Agent mentions `nvidia-smi pmon -c 1` when multiple Python processes may exist

evals.name: checkpoint-safe-cancel-resume
evals.prompt: I need to stop a running fine-tuning job and resume later without losing progress.
evals.expected_output: Agent waits for a durable checkpoint/progress artifact, verifies files and log lines, cancels only at a safe boundary, then resumes with a descriptive job name using the existing resume logic.

assertions.text: Agent waits for or verifies a durable checkpoint/progress artifact before cancellation
Agent checks both disk state and log confirmation before relying on resume
Agent does NOT recommend cancelling at an arbitrary point when progress consistency matters
Agent uses a descriptive incremental job name for the resumed run
